document.title: Development of a Digital X-ray Imaging Module for Portable High-Resolution Imaging Applications
document.documentDate: May 2005; received 19 January 2005; final form 10 March 2005

sourceSummary.oneLine: Journal article describing development and image-performance evaluation of a CMOS-based portable digital X-ray imaging module.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Not an official regulatory document; usable as literature evidence for clinical evaluation or design validation context where source traceability is retained.
keyPoints: The imaging module consists of a commercially-available CMOS photosensor array, a Gd2O2S:Tb scintillator, a readout IC board, a battery-operated X-ray generator, and GUI software.
Performance evaluation was presented in terms of system response, CNR, MTF, NPS, and DQE.
The conclusion reports CNR at 30 mR of 34.3, spatial frequency at 10% MTF of about 9.0, NPS at zero frequency of 4.4 × 10−5, and DQE at zero frequency of 0.3.

concepts.name: Digital X-ray imaging module
concepts.evidence: We have developed a digital X-ray imaging module based on a CMOS photosensor array for portable high-resolution imaging applications and evaluated its image performance.

concepts.name: Portable digital radiography
concepts.evidence: potential application as a portable digital X-ray imaging device for use in emergency medical vehicles or at remote sites

concepts.name: CMOS photosensor array
concepts.evidence: The imaging system consists of a CMOS photosensor array directly coupled with a Gd2O2S : Tb scintillator

concepts.name: Gd2O2S:Tb scintillator
concepts.evidence: The photodiode arrays were directly coupled with a 46.3-μm-thick Gd2O2S : Tb scintillator (Min-R, Kodak).

concepts.name: Contrast-to-noise ratio
concepts.evidence: The evaluation was done in terms of the system response, the CNR, the MTF, the NPS, and the DQE.

concepts.name: Modulation transfer function
concepts.evidence: One-dimensional presampled MTF was measured using the angled-edge technique to characterize the resolution properties of the imaging system.

concepts.name: Noise power spectrum
concepts.evidence: The system NPS was measured from uniformly irradiated radiographs by using a two-dimensional Fourier analysis method.

concepts.name: Detective quantum efficiency
concepts.evidence: The detective quantum efficiency for the system was deduced from the measured MTF, NPS, and exposure

evidence.claim: The source is a May 2005 Journal of the Korean Physical Society article titled "Development of a Digital X-ray Imaging Module for Portable High-Resolution Imaging Applications."
evidence.evidenceText: Journal of the Korean Physical Society, Vol. 46, No. 5, May 2005, pp. 1096∼1101 Development of a Digital X-ray Imaging Module for Portable High-Resolution Imaging Applications
evidence.pageTarget: Development of a Digital X-ray Imaging Module for Portable High-Resolution Imaging Applications

evidence.claim: The article was received on 19 January 2005 and in final form on 10 March 2005.
evidence.pageTarget: Development of a Digital X-ray Imaging Module for Portable High-Resolution Imaging Applications

evidence.claim: The developed module was based on a CMOS photosensor array for portable high-resolution imaging applications.
evidence.evidenceText: We have developed a digital X-ray imaging module based on a CMOS photosensor array for portable high-resolution imaging applications and evaluated its image performance.

evidence.claim: The imaging module included a CMOS photosensor array, Gd2O2S:Tb scintillator, readout IC board, battery-operated X-ray generator, and GUI software.
evidence.evidenceText: The imaging module consists of a commercially-available CMOS photosensor array with a 48 μm × 48 μm pixel size and a 49.2 mm × 49.3 mm active area, a conventional Gd2O2S:Tb scintillator (Min-R, Kodak), a controlling readout IC board, a battery-operated X-ray generator (60 kVp, < 1 mA), and GUI software.

evidence.claim: The CMOS photosensor array used was a commercially available RadEyeTM 2 premium-grade sensor.
evidence.evidenceText: As a digital X-ray imaging sensor, a commercially-available CMOS photosensor array (RadEyeT M 2) of premium grade (no line defect) was introduced to our imaging system

evidence.claim: The RadEyeTM 2 sensor had dual silicon photodiode arrays, 48 μm pixel size, and a 512 × 1024 pixel matrix.
evidence.evidenceText: It contained dual silicon photodiode arrays with a pixel size of 48 μm and 512 × 1024 pixel matrix, along with associated circuitry for scanning and readout.

evidence.claim: The photodiode arrays were directly coupled with a 46.3 μm thick Gd2O2S:Tb scintillator, Min-R by Kodak.
evidence.evidenceText: The photodiode arrays were directly coupled with a 46.3-μm-thick Gd2O2S : Tb scintillator (Min-R, Kodak).

evidence.claim: The prototype readout IC board included amplifier AD8022, ADC ADS805E, FPGA ACEX, SRAM memory, and USB chip FT245BM.
evidence.evidenceText: The IC board consists of an amplifier (AD8022) for buffering and amplifying small image signals, an analog-to-digital converter (ADS805E, 12 bit, 20 MHz), and components for image signal control and transmission. The acquired image data were controlled by using a FPGA control chip (ACEX, Altera) and were stored into a SRAM memory (512 kBx4, Samsung Electronics). An USB chip (FT245BM, 1-MB data-transfer rate) was also used

evidence.claim: The battery-operated X-ray generator consisted of an X-ray tube and a 16 V, 6 A battery-operated high-voltage power supply module.
evidence.evidenceText: It consists of an X-ray tube (20 ∼ 60 kVp, < 1 mA, 800-μm focal size) and a battery-operated (16 V, 6 A) HVPS (high-voltage power supply) module.

evidence.claim: Performance evaluation was conducted with a 46.3 μm thick Min-R scintillator using experimental measurements and acquired X-ray images.
evidence.evidenceText: Performance evaluation for our digital X-ray imaging system was performed with a 46.3-μm-thick Min-R scintillator by using experimental measurements and acquired X-ray images.

evidence.claim: The evaluation metrics were system response, CNR, MTF, NPS, and DQE.
evidence.evidenceText: The evaluation was done in terms of the system response, the CNR, the MTF, the NPS, and the DQE.

evidence.claim: The measured spatial frequency at 10% MTF was about 9.0 LP/mm.
evidence.evidenceText: As Fig. 12 shows, the spatial frequency measured at 10 % MTF was about 9.0 LP/mm

evidence.claim: The normalized NPS value at zero frequency was about 4.4 × 10−5.
evidence.evidenceText: the NPS value at zero frequency was about 4.4 × 10−5

evidence.claim: The DQE value at zero frequency was about 0.3.
evidence.evidenceText: the DQE value at zero frequency is about 0.3

evidence.claim: The article conclusion states that the module gave promising image performances for many high-resolution imaging applications.
evidence.evidenceText: In terms of system response, resolution, noise, and detective quantum efficiency, the portable digital X-ray imaging module based on the CMOS array with the Min-R scintillator gave promising image performances for many high-resolution imaging applications.

evidence.claim: The image system was described as useful for potential portable applications in emergency medical vehicles and remote sites.
evidence.evidenceText: The image system we developed in this study will be useful for potential portable applications, such as those in emergency medical vehicles and at remote sites.

reviewFlags.reason: Source is a journal article, not an official RA or regulatory authority document.
No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is stated in the source text.
No revision or version identifier is stated in the source text.
Product signal HAD/A/F 1417-1717 appears only in supplied metadata and is not found in the source text.
Some OCR spacing artifacts appear in model text, such as RadEyeT M 2; normalized as RadEyeTM 2 with medium confidence where used.
